readline/get_begidx.name: get_begidx
readline/get_begidx.description: Get the beginning or ending index of the completion scope. These indexes are the start and end arguments passed to the rl_attempted_completion_function callback of the underlying library. The values may be different in the same input editing scenario based on the underlying C readline implementation. Ex: libedit is known to behave differently than libreadline.

info.excerpt: Get the beginning or ending index of the completion scope. These indexes are the start and end arguments passed to the rl_attempted_completion_function callback of the underlying library. The values m...

readline/get_completer.name: get_completer
readline/get_completer.description: Get the completer function, or None if no completer function has been set.

info.excerpt: Get the completer function, or None if no completer function has been set.

readline/get_completion_type.name: get_completion_type
readline/get_completion_type.description: Get the type of completion being attempted. This returns the rl_completion_type variable in the underlying library as an integer.

info.excerpt: Get the type of completion being attempted. This returns the rl_completion_type variable in the underlying library as an integer.

readline/get_current_history_length.name: get_current_history_length
readline/get_current_history_length.description: Return the number of items currently in the history. (This is different from get_history_length(), which returns the maximum number of lines that will be written to a history file.)

info.excerpt: Return the number of items currently in the history. (This is different from get_history_length(), which returns the maximum number of lines that will be written to a history file.)

readline/get_history_item.name: get_history_item
readline/get_history_item.description: Return the current contents of history item at index. The item index is one-based. This calls history_get() in the underlying library.

info.excerpt: Return the current contents of history item at index. The item index is one-based. This calls history_get() in the underlying library.

readline/get_history_length.name: get_history_length
readline/get_history_length.description: Set or return the desired number of lines to save in the history file. The write_history_file() function uses this value to truncate the history file, by calling history_truncate_file() in the underlying library. Negative values imply unlimited history file size.

info.excerpt: Set or return the desired number of lines to save in the history file. The write_history_file() function uses this value to truncate the history file, by calling history_truncate_file() in the underly...

readline/get_line_buffer.name: get_line_buffer
readline/get_line_buffer.description: Return the current contents of the line buffer (rl_line_buffer in the underlying library).

info.excerpt: Return the current contents of the line buffer (rl_line_buffer in the underlying library).
